message:
feat(resolv): port libresolv ns_parse_ttl + ns_format_ttl + ns_datetosecs (bd-xrqam)

Three more libresolv low-level helpers for DNS TTL and date parsing.

  - ns_parse_ttl(src, *dst): parse a DNS TTL string like "1d2h3m4s"
    into u32 seconds. Grammar (case-insensitive): (number unit?)+ where
    unit is W/D/H/M/S; missing trailing unit defaults to seconds.
    Multipliers: W=604800, D=86400, H=3600, M=60, S=1. Returns 0 on
    success, -1 on syntax error or u32 overflow. Empty input returns 0
    matching the BIND9 reference loop semantics.
  - ns_format_ttl(secs, dst, dstlen): format a u32 seconds count as a
    TTL string with greedy decomposition into weeks/days/hours/minutes/
    seconds. Single-unit results are uppercase ("1M", "1D"); multi-unit
    results are lowercase ("1d1m", "1h1m1s") matching the BIND9 ref.
    Returns the length written (excluding NUL) or -1 on overflow.
  - ns_datetosecs(cp, *errp): parse a 14-char "YYYYMMDDHHMMSS" UTC
    string into seconds since epoch via libc::timegm. Sets *errp = 1 on
    parse error else 0. NULL errp tolerated.

Tests: 10 new abi tests covering unit parsing, syntax errors, overflow
guards, single-vs-multi-unit casing, format→parse round-trip, buffer
sizing failures, valid UTC parses (epoch + 2024-01-01), and rejection
of malformed dates (wrong length, non-digit, out-of-range month).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-3lw9b","title":"PORT: libresolv ns_get/put16/32 + ns_samename/samedomain/subdomain/makecanon","description":"libresolv ns_get16/ns_get32/ns_put16/ns_put32 (big-endian byte read/write); ns_samename(a, b) (case-insensitive name compare with optional trailing-dot tolerance); ns_samedomain(a, b) (a == b or a ends with .b); ns_subdomain(a, b) (samedomain && !samename); ns_makecanon(src, dst, dstsiz) (copy with trailing dot ensured). Returns 1/0 boolean for the comparators, 0/-1 with errno=EMSGSIZE for makecanon. These are the simplest libresolv parsing helpers and currently absent from our resolv_abi.rs (the high-level resolver lives there but the low-level ns_* helpers do not). Eight functions total.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:33:13.927262472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:33:13.927262472Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3mam","title":"bd-1x3 subtask: Workload-ranked top-N API enablement wave planning","description":"Background:
- Stub elimination must be driven by real workload impact and release-level goals.

Goal:
- Define top-N API enablement waves from workload traces and support matrix obligations.

Deliverables:
1) Workload-driven ranking of missing/partial APIs.
2) Wave plan with explicit success criteria and evidence requirements.
3) Integration with existing open subsystem tasks (setjmp/TLS/threading/hard-parts).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Wave order is data-driven and dependency-correct.
- Progress is measurable through objective burn-down metrics.

Verification & Logging:
- Ranking pipeline tests.
- Structured logs for ranking inputs, scores, and wave assignments.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberStone","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.133627294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:23.372513263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:23.372488627Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic workload-ranked top-N API wave plan generator, artifact, gate, and harness test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","roadmap","stubs","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1003,"issue_id":"bd-3mam","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented bd-3mam workload-ranked top-N API enablement wave planning.\n\nDelivered:\n- scripts/generate_workload_api_wave_plan.py (new deterministic ranking/wave generator; top-N scoring from workload blockers + support_matrix + callthrough census dependencies)\n- tests/conformance/workload_api_wave_plan.v1.json (new generated artifact; top 25 symbol ranking, module ranking, dependency-aware wave plan, integration hooks for setjmp/TLS/threading/hard-parts)\n- scripts/check_workload_api_wave_plan.sh (new gate: reproducibility, ranking consistency, acyclic wave dependencies, integration-hook presence, summary consistency, structured log/report emission)\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/workload_api_wave_plan_test.rs (new harness gate test)\n\nVerification:\n- scripts/check_workload_api_wave_plan.sh PASS\n- python3 scripts/generate_workload_api_wave_plan.py --output tests/conformance/workload_api_wave_plan.v1.json --top-n 25 --check PASS\n- python3 -m py_compile scripts/generate_workload_api_wave_plan.py PASS\n- bash -n scripts/check_workload_api_wave_plan.sh PASS\n- python3 -m json.tool tests/conformance/workload_api_wave_plan.v1.json PASS\n\nNote on cargo tests:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test workload_api_wave_plan_test currently blocked by unrelated upstream pthread compile break (E0603 private syscall::raw in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs) outside this bead scope.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-xpgak","title":"xdr_pointer / xdr_reference: no depth limit on caller-supplied recursive procs","description":"## Defect

`xdr_pointer` (rpc_abi.rs:1399) and `xdr_reference` (rpc_abi.rs:1362)
take a caller-supplied per-element xdr_proc. If the caller passes a
proc that recursively invokes xdr_pointer on a nested field (e.g.,
a generated stub for an XDR type with a self-referential
optional pointer like `struct tree { tree *left; tree *right;}`),
attacker-supplied wire data of repeated `more=1` flags walks the
recursion arbitrarily deep — same DoS class as bd-lc5ss but
caller-controlled rather than library-internal.

bd-lc5ss fixed the library's own internal recursive type
(xdr_pmaplist) by rewriting iteratively. That fix doesn't help
caller-supplied recursive types; the library has no per-thread
depth tracker on xdr_pointer/xdr_reference at all.

## Fix

Add a `XDR_RECURSE_DEPTH` thread_local Cell<usize> with a hard cap
(e.g. 256). xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit,
and returns XDR_FALSE if the counter exceeds the cap. This is
defense-in-depth: callers using rpcgen-generated stubs with
sensible types never approach the cap, but a malicious wire stream
fed to a vulnerable caller is bounded by the depth limit instead
of by available stack.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3). The library-internal recursive type was fixed by
bd-lc5ss; this remaining attack surface requires a vulnerable
CALLER (one that passes a recursive proc), so it's a smaller blast
radius than bd-lc5ss but still a real wire-controlled DoS for any
RPC server that exposes recursive XDR types.","notes":"Fixed in 9d326b98: XDR_REFERENCE_DEPTH thread_local Cell<u32> with hard cap of 256. xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit. Added regression test xdr_reference_depth_limit_refuses_deep_recursive_wire_without_overflow with 1024-deep recursive RecursiveNode chain. 90/90 xdr_abi_test pass; clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T02:00:51.865514841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xrmnr","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc math.h core diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped in commit 0a96670b. 15 functions (sqrt/fabs/floor/ceil/fmod exact + sin/cos/tan/atan2/exp/log/log2/log10/pow/sinh/cosh/tanh ULP-tolerant), 9 tests. Custom IEEE-754 classification + ULP-distance comparator. Zero divergences at 4-ULP tolerance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T08:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T08:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T08:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences against glibc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}
+{"id":"bd-xrqam","title":"PORT: libresolv ns_parse_ttl + ns_format_ttl + ns_datetosecs","description":"libresolv ns_parse_ttl(src, *dst) parses a DNS TTL string into a u32 seconds count. Grammar (case-insensitive): TTL := (number unit?)+ where unit is W/D/H/M/S and a missing trailing unit defaults to seconds. Multipliers: W=604800, D=86400, H=3600, M=60, S=1. ns_format_ttl(secs, dst, dstlen) formats a u32 seconds count as a uppercase TTL string greedy-decomposed by weeks/days/hours/minutes/seconds (60 -> "1M", 86460 -> "1D1M", 0 -> "0S"). Returns the length written, -1 on overflow. ns_datetosecs(cp, *errp) parses a 14-char "YYYYMMDDHHMMSS" UTC string into seconds since epoch via timegm; sets *errp = 1 on parse error else 0. All three live in resolv_abi.rs. Use checked arithmetic so overflow returns -1.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:42:57.083716842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:42:57.083716842Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xsc3","title":"syscall: mount_setattr AT_EMPTY_PATH should not override NULL pathname EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for mount_setattr(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_EMPTY_PATH, NULL, 0). Add ABI regression coverage proving AT_EMPTY_PATH does not bypass the NULL pathname contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:46.771233629Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:59:37.158371288Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:59:37.155879079Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xvdk3","title":"syscall: renameat2 null newpath should return EFAULT exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, ".", AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the null-destination-path contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:09.402096663Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:07:22.456654477Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:07:22.454530016Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xx2j1","title":"printf %n in hardened mode denies writes to untracked pointers (stack, external heap) breaking POSIX contract","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs:3142-3157\` in the %n
path:

    if mode.heals_enabled() || matches!(decision.action, Repair(_)) {
        if let Some(rem) = crate::malloc_abi::known_remaining(ptr_val) {
            if rem < size {
                should_write = false;
                ...
            }
        } else {
            // Pointer not tracked by our allocator.
            should_write = false;      ← BUG
            ...
        }
    }

When \`known_remaining(ptr_val)\` returns \`None\` (pointer isn't in
our tracked allocator — typical for a stack var like
\`let mut count: i32\` in caller code, or an external malloc),
the heal logic sets \`should_write = false\` unconditionally.

Result: in hardened mode, every %n call with a caller-provided
\`int*\` that doesn't happen to land in our tracked heap is
silently skipped. Callers see their int unchanged instead of
the byte-count-so-far that POSIX mandates.

Reproduced by fuzz_printf's fuzz_percent_n_abi:
- Format \"abc%n:%s\" with stack-allocated count
- Expected: count == 3 (bytes \"abc\" written before %n)
- Observed: count == -1 (harness init value, never overwritten)

## Fix

Absence of allocator tracking is not evidence of danger. The
membrane's \`decide()\` already ran; if it returned non-Deny, the
pointer should be writable. Only set \`should_write = false\` in
the inner branch where \`rem < size\` is explicit.

    if let Some(rem) = crate::malloc_abi::known_remaining(ptr_val) {
        if rem < size { should_write = false; ... }
    }
    // else: untracked allocation, rely on decide() verdict

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P2). %n is security-sensitive (CVE-classic format string
vector) and most production callers don't use it on attacker-
controlled format strings. But programs that legitimately use %n
(e.g., parsing APIs that need consumed-byte count) get wrong
results under hardened mode.","notes":"

Fixed in 6d29fb13: removed the else-branch in the heal-path that set should_write=false when known_remaining returned None. Untracked allocations (stack, external heap) now honor the membrane's non-Deny verdict. POSIX %n contract holds under hardened mode.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:07:40.665097128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:14:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:14:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
